name: Beignets
recipeInstructions:
1. Proof the yeast.
2. Scald the milk; stir in the sugar, shortening, and salt. Cook the mixture to a temperature between 105 and 115 degrees.
3. Add the milk mixture, egg, and 2 cups flour to the yeast mixture, mixing well.
4. Gradually stir in enough remaining flour to make a soft dough. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface, and knead until smooth and elastic (8 to 10 minutes).
5. Place in a well-greased bowl, turning to grease the top. Cover and let rise about 1 hour, or until doubled.
6. Punch dough down; turn out onto a lightly floured surface. Roll dough into a rectangle measuring 12 inches by 10 inches; cut into squares measuring 2 inches on each side.
7. Place on a floured surface; cover and let rise in a warm place for an additional 30 minutes, or until doubled in bulk.
8. Heat oil to 375 degrees. Drop 3 to 4 beignets at a time into the hot oil. Cook for 1 minute on each side or until golden brown.
9. Drain on paper towels and sprinkle with powdered sugar.
